user_query:
I’m putting together a ranked list of my favourite North London derby stories (Arsenal vs Spurs) from the assets I’ve saved locally. Please use only the files in this workspace (no Internet). Do the following: 1) Discover and read the input files by scanning the workspace: the match stats CSV and the stories HTML are under input/, the scoring rules are under config/weights.yaml, and team name normalisation lives in scripts/parser.py. Log every file you access under input/, config/, and scripts/ into output/discovered_files.json. 2) Identify derby matches in input/matches.csv by normalising team names using TEAM_ALIASES from scripts/parser.py (e.g., map “Spurs” and “Tottenham” to “Tottenham Hotspur” and “Gunners” to “Arsenal”). Treat any match where either side is Arsenal or Tottenham Hotspur as a North London derby. 3) Link each derby match to its story in input/stories.html via the article’s data-match-id attribute. From each article, extract: - tags: the text of each
  • within
      ; - summary: the first sentence of the

      text (clip to ≤140 characters if needed). 4) Compute drama_score per match using config/weights.yaml. Use exact keys and values from weights.yaml: - tag_points = sum of keyword_points for tags that exactly match keys in weights.keyword_points (ignore tags not present); - goal_diff_component = max(0, 2 - abs(home_goals - away_goals)) * weights.goal_diff_weight; - last_minute_component = 1.0 if winning_goal_minute exists and is ≥ weights.last_minute_threshold, else 0.0; - drama_score = round(tag_points + goal_diff_component + last_minute_component, 2). 5) Classify each match: - Legendary if drama_score ≥ classification.legendary_min - Memorable if drama_score ≥ classification.memorable_min and < classification.legendary_min - Routine otherwise. 6) Rank all derby matches by drama_score (descending), breaking ties by date (descending). Deliverables: - output/derby_rankings.csv with columns: match_id,date,competition,home_team,away_team,home_goals,away_goals,drama_score,class,summary. Use canonical team names per TEAM_ALIASES in scripts/parser.py. - output/discovered_files.json listing the relative paths of all files you inspected under input/, config/, and scripts/. Everything should be derived from the provided files so the results are reproducible. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
